meta.title: Poetry Collection — Vocabulary Set 1
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'Poetry Collection' — all 16 words (might, deep, skip, zoom, brought, great, box, crawled, cardboard, cave, dragon, carved, squishy, scoop, moat, decorate).

questions.question: What does 'zoom' mean?
options: To move very slowly and carefully
questions.explanation: 'Zoom' means to move very fast. In the poem 'Me!', a bird can 'zoom from tree to tree' — it flies very quickly.

questions.question: What does 'skip' mean?
options: To jump lightly from foot to foot while moving forward
questions.explanation: 'Skip' means to jump lightly from foot to foot as you move. The poem says people can skip — something fish and birds cannot do!

questions.question: 'Squishy' describes something that is soft and a little wet when touched.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Squishy' means soft and wet. In 'Sandcastle,' the sand is described as 'soft and squishy, wet and brown' — like wet sand feels when you squeeze it.

questions.question: A _______ is a ditch of water that goes all the way around a castle to protect it.
questions.hint: A water ditch around a castle
questions.explanation: A 'moat' is a deep water ditch built around a castle. In 'Sandcastle,' the children make a moat around their sand castle — 'a moat surrounds it all.'

questions.question: What does 'cave' mean?
options: A hollow space inside a rock or hill
questions.explanation: A 'cave' is a hollow space inside a rock or hill. In 'The Box,' the child imagines the cardboard box is a 'cardboard cave' — it's dark and hollow like a cave.

questions.question: Dad 'carved' a hole in the box. What does 'carved' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Carved' is the past tense of 'carve' — to cut carefully with a tool. Dad carved a little hole in the box to make a window for the child.

questions.question: The child _______ inside the box — she moved on her hands and knees to get in.
questions.hint: Moved slowly on hands and knees
questions.explanation: 'Crawled' means moved on hands and knees. The poem says the child 'crawled inside a cardboard cave' — getting into the box on hands and knees.

questions.question: 'Cardboard' is a thick, stiff type of paper used to make boxes.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Cardboard' is a thick, stiff material made from paper. Boxes, cereal packets, and shipping containers are often made from cardboard.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they VERBS (action words) or NOUNS (things)?
questions.explanation: 'Scoop' (to dig up), 'zoom' (to move fast), and 'decorate' (to make pretty) are actions (verbs). 'Dragon,' 'moat,' and 'cave' are things (nouns).

questions.question: The children _______ the sandcastle with shells and sticks to make it look nice.
questions.hint: To make something look pretty by adding things
questions.explanation: 'Decorate' means to make something look nicer by adding things to it. In 'Sandcastle,' the children decorate their castle to make it beautiful.

questions.question: In the poem, the child says she 'might' have fun being a fish. What does 'might' mean?
options: It is possible, but not certain
questions.explanation: 'Might' means something is possible but not certain. 'It might be fun' — the speaker is not sure, but it is possible. It is used to imagine possibilities.

questions.question: Use 'scoop' correctly in a sentence:
options: I scoop the door and walk in.
We use a shovel to scoop sand into the bucket.
questions.explanation: 'Scoop' means to pick up something with a curved tool or your hands. 'Scoop sand into the bucket' — using a scooping motion to fill the bucket.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence from 'The Box':
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Dad carved a little hole.' Dad used a tool to carefully cut a small hole in the box, which the child imagined as a cave entrance.

questions.question: A 'dragon' is a real animal you can find in a jungle.
questions.explanation: A 'dragon' is a mythical creature — it exists only in stories, poems, and imagination. In 'The Box,' the child imagines fighting a dragon because she is using her imagination.

questions.question: Sort these words — do they describe something you can TOUCH AND FEEL (physical) or something that involves IMAGINATION (imaginary)?
questions.explanation: 'Squishy,' 'cardboard,' 'moat,' and 'cave' are all physical things you can touch or see. 'Dragon' is imaginary (it doesn't exist), and 'might' expresses an imaginary possibility.
